title: Add spaced repetition review system using FSRS algorithm for OS concepts
description: LAYER 6 RETENTION. Ebbinghaus forgetting curve: students forget 80% within a week. FSRS (Free Spaced Repetition Scheduler) reduces review load 20-30% vs SM-2 while maintaining 87-89% retention. Track each OS concept individually (e.g., 'FCFS scheduling', 'LRU eviction logic', 'Coffman conditions'). Schedule review mini-challenges at personalized intervals. When a concept is due for review, show it in the dashboard 'Review Due' section. Review = interactive challenge (from practice system), not passive re-reading. Frontend-only with localStorage persistence. Open-source FSRS implementations available on GitHub.
acceptanceCriteria: Each concept tracks: stability, difficulty, retrievability (FSRS Three Component Model)
After completing a practice challenge, FSRS schedules the next review
Dashboard shows 'X concepts due for review today'
Review session: 6 questions across 3 concepts (Khan Academy format)
Correct answers increase interval; wrong answers decrease
All data persisted in localStorage (architex-srs key)
Works without user account (anonymous spaced repetition)

title: Add shareable URL state — encode simulation parameters + step in URL
description: LAYER 9 DISCOVERY. A user should be able to share a specific simulation state: 'Look at this deadlock I found' → URL encodes: concept, algorithm, input parameters, current step index. When recipient opens the URL, the exact simulation state is reproduced. Use URL search params or compressed base64 state. The existing to-url.ts export utility can be extended. Frontend-only.
acceptanceCriteria: Share button on each concept panel copies URL to clipboard
URL encodes: concept, algorithm, input parameters (processes/ref string/etc.)
Optionally encodes current step index for step-through concepts
Opening the URL reproduces the exact simulation state
URL is human-readable where possible: /os/cpu-scheduling?algo=fcfs&processes=P1:0:4,P2:1:3
Fallback: compressed state via lz-string (already in dependencies) for complex inputs

title: Add embeddable widget — iframe snippet for embedding OS simulations in blog posts
description: LAYER 9 DISCOVERY. Professors, bloggers, and tutorial writers should be able to embed an interactive OS simulation in their website. Add an 'Embed' button that generates an iframe snippet: <iframe src='https://architex.dev/embed/os/cpu-scheduling?algo=fcfs&processes=...' width='100%' height='400'/>. The embed route renders a minimal version (no sidebar, no properties panel — just canvas + controls). Frontend-only.
acceptanceCriteria: Embed button on each concept panel generates iframe code
/embed/os/[concept] route renders minimal simulation view
Embed is responsive (fills container width)
Embed includes 'Powered by Architex' link at bottom
Embed loads quickly (<2s TTI) by skipping non-essential components
Embed respects dark/light theme of host page (prefers-color-scheme)

title: Add auto-generated OG images per OS concept for social sharing
description: LAYER 9 DISCOVERY. When someone shares an OS concept URL on Twitter/LinkedIn, the preview card should show a beautiful, informative image — not a generic site logo. The existing /api/og endpoint supports title, type, and difficulty params. Generate concept-specific OG images: 'CPU Scheduling — FCFS vs SJF vs Round Robin' with a mini Gantt chart preview. This drives click-through from social media. Frontend + existing API.
acceptanceCriteria: Each /os/[concept] page has unique OG image via /api/og endpoint
OG image shows: concept name, algorithm count, mini-visualization preview
Twitter card: large image summary format
LinkedIn: article format with image
Image is cache-friendly (generated once, served from edge)
Verified with Twitter Card Validator and LinkedIn Post Inspector

title: Add flashcard generation — exportable review cards for each OS concept
description: LAYER 6 RETENTION. After exploring a concept, the user can click 'Generate Flashcards' to get 5-10 review cards. Cards cover: key definitions ('What is FCFS?'), complexity facts ('Time complexity of LRU?'), comparison questions ('Mutex vs Semaphore — key difference?'), and application questions ('Which scheduling algorithm does Linux use?'). Export as: copy to clipboard (Anki-compatible format), or download as CSV. Frontend-only.
acceptanceCriteria: Flashcard button in each concept's Learn tab or properties panel
5-10 cards per concept covering definitions, complexity, comparisons, applications
Cards are Q&A format: front (question) + back (answer)
Export to clipboard in Anki-compatible tab-separated format
Export as CSV download
Cards are concept-specific — FCFS cards differ from MLFQ cards

title: Add Export as GIF/MP4 — record simulation animation for social media sharing
description: LAYER 9 DISCOVERY + LAYER 10 INNOVATION. The 'screenshot test' showed no visualization is shareable. Add MediaRecorder-based capture: user clicks 'Record', runs simulation, clicks 'Stop'. Output: GIF or MP4 of the visualization area. This enables: professors embedding in slides, students sharing 'look what I learned', and social media viral moments (Belady's Anomaly GIF would be highly shareable). Uses MediaRecorder API (no backend needed).
acceptanceCriteria: Record button in each concept's toolbar
Records the canvas/visualization area (not the entire page)
Output format: WebM (native) with option to convert to GIF via client-side library
Recording indicator visible during capture
Maximum 30 seconds to prevent memory issues
Download button after recording stops
Respects prefers-reduced-motion (no recording if animations disabled)

title: Add multiplayer algorithm race — two users trace the same algorithm competitively via WebRTC
description: LAYER 10 INNOVATION — FIRST IN THE WORLD. Two users get the same input data. Both trace the algorithm manually (Be the Algorithm mode from OSC-119). First to correctly trace all steps wins. Uses WebRTC for peer-to-peer (no backend server needed). Shareable via room code URL. This combines the manual tracing practice feature with competitive motivation. Research: competitive learning increases engagement 2-3x (Kahoot data).
acceptanceCriteria: Race button creates a room with shareable URL code
Second user joins via URL
Both see same input data and algorithm
Each traces steps independently (clicks which process runs next, etc.)
Progress bar shows both players' advancement
First to correctly complete all steps wins
Results: time taken, accuracy, mistakes made
Works peer-to-peer via WebRTC (no server needed)

title: Add 'Learning Wrapped' — annual summary of what you learned, Spotify Wrapped style
description: LAYER 6 RETENTION + LAYER 7 COMMUNITY. At year-end (or on demand), generate a beautiful shareable summary: 'In 2026, you explored 42 OS concepts, ran 156 simulations, maintained a 12-day streak, and mastered 8 algorithms. Your strongest area: Memory Management. Your nemesis: Deadlock. You predicted 73% of scheduling decisions correctly.' Generates a shareable image card. Drives social sharing and return visits. Frontend-only using existing progress data.
acceptanceCriteria: Accessible from dashboard or profile
Shows: concepts explored, simulations run, streak stats, accuracy stats
Highlights: strongest topic, weakest topic, most-used algorithm
Fun stat: 'You traced 847 scheduling decisions — that's more than a Linux kernel handles in 0.001 seconds'
Generates shareable image card (html2canvas or OG-style)
Share button for Twitter/LinkedIn with pre-filled text

title: Add achievement badges for OS learning milestones
description: LAYER 6 RETENTION. Achievements tap into accomplishment drive (Octalysis Core Drive 2). Design 15-20 badges: 'First Run' (run any simulation), 'Compare Master' (use Compare All in 3 concepts), 'Belady's Discoverer' (see the anomaly), 'Deadlock Detective' (detect 5 deadlocks), 'Memory Wizard' (100% TLB hit rate prediction), 'Streak Champion' (7-day streak), 'Speed Demon' (complete all steps in under 30s), 'Full Explorer' (visit all 6 concepts). Badges displayed on profile and shareable.
acceptanceCriteria: 15-20 badges across categories: exploration, mastery, streak, speed, completion
Badge earned shows toast notification with animation
Badge gallery accessible from profile or module header
Each badge has: name, icon, description, unlock condition, date earned
Shareable: click badge → copy share image
Badges persisted in localStorage (architex-achievements key)

title: Add social proof counters — 'X students explored CPU Scheduling this week'
description: LAYER 7 COMMUNITY. Social proof increases trust and engagement. Add counters showing aggregate usage: 'CPU Scheduling: 12,847 simulations run this month', 'Page Replacement: 8,234 students explored'. Counters can be real (backed by analytics API) or estimated (based on traffic data). Even estimated counters create social proof. Display subtly in sidebar or concept headers.
acceptanceCriteria: Each concept shows aggregate usage counter
Counter format: '12.8K simulations run' or '8.2K students explored'
Counter updates periodically (real: from API, estimated: from seed + time)
Displayed subtly — not competing with primary UI
Landing page shows total: '50,000+ students learning OS concepts'
